description: Language-agnostic quality gate hooks. Patterns are configured per project, not hardcoded.

anti_patterns.description: Patterns that should NOT appear in committed code. Configure per project.

rules.name: debug-artifacts
rules.description: Debug code left in source files

rules.name: hardcoded-secrets
rules.message: Never hardcode secrets. Use environment variables.

rules.name: test-skip-banned
rules.description: .skip() and .todo() produce tests that catch nothing
rules.message: Use xfail or assert broken value with // BUG: comment instead of .skip()/.todo()

rules.name: empty-catch-blocks
rules.message: Never swallow errors. Log + rethrow or handle explicitly.

rules.name: god-functions
rules.message: See rules/coding-standards.md — functions must be under 40 lines

web_specific.name: hardcoded-colors
web_specific.description: Tailwind color classes instead of design system tokens
web_specific.message: Use CSS variables / design tokens instead of hardcoded color classes

web_specific.name: hardcoded-strings-ui
web_specific.description: Hardcoded user-facing strings instead of i18n keys
web_specific.message: Use i18n translation keys for user-facing text

api_specific.name: hardcoded-urls
api_specific.description: Hardcoded API URLs instead of config
api_specific.message: Use environment variables for API URLs

cli_specific.name: hardcoded-paths
cli_specific.description: Hardcoded file paths instead of config
cli_specific.message: Use config or env vars for file paths

pre_commit.description: Run before git commit. Uses {files} placeholder for staged files.
pre_commit.command_template: bash -c 'FILES=$(git diff --cached --name-only -- {filter}); if [ -n "$FILES" ]; then FOUND=$(echo "$FILES" | xargs grep -l "{pattern}" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FOUND" ]; then echo "❌ {name}: {message}"; echo "$FOUND"; exit 1; fi; fi; echo "✅ {name}: clean"; exit 0'

post_edit.description: Run after editing a file. Uses {file} for the edited file path.

checks.description: External command checks run by code_review.py. Projects fill this in with their stack-specific tools. {files} is replaced with the space-separated list of matching files.

examples.name: lint
examples.message: Linter errors must be fixed before committing

examples.name: typecheck
examples.message: TypeScript type errors must be resolved

code_review.description: Automated code review hook (Pass 2). Run via: python stacks/hooks/code_review.py

modes.pre-commit: Check staged files before git commit

code_review.json_verdict: Use --json flag to get structured verdict for Claude stop hook integration
